title: Improved process for the manufacture of r-6-hydroxy-8-[1-hydroxy-2-[2-(4-methoxyphenyl)-1,1-dimethylethylaminoethyl]-2h-1,4-benzoxazin-3(4h)-one hydrochloride
abstract: The present invention provides an improved process for the manufacture ( R )-6- hydroxy-8-[1-hydroxy-2-[2-(4-methoxyphenyl)-1,1-dimethyl-ethylamino-ethyl]-2H-1,4- benzoxazin-3(4H)-one, in high purity and high yield, through the use of 1,1-dimethyl-2- (4-methoxyphenyl)ethyl amine L-tartrate salt, 1,1-dimethyl-2-(4-methoxyphenyl)ethyl amine maletate salt or the camphorsulfonate salt of intermediate (4). The invention also relates to sais salts, to processes for preparing them and to their use for the manufacture of ( R )-6-hydroxy-8-[1-hydroxy-2-[2-(4-methoxyphenyl)-1,1-dimethyl- ethylamino-ethyl]-2H-1,4-benzoxazin-3(4H)-one or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof.
claims:
Claims 1. A camphorsulfonate salt of intermediate (4), wherein the PG is a hydroxyl protecting group, preferably an aralkyl group, more preferably C Cioalkyl substituted by a C 6 - C 10 aryl group, more p l. 2. The camphorsulfonate salt according to claim 1 , wherein the camphorsulfonic acid is the (R)-(-)-camphorsulfonic acid. 3. The camphorsulfonate salt according to claim 1 , wherein the camphorsulfonic acid is the (S)-(+)-camphorsulfonic acid. 4. The camphorsulfonate salt according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the molar ratio of intermediate (4) to the camphorsulfonic acid is 1 :1. 5. The camphorsulfonate salt according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the hydroxyl protecting group PG is benzyl. 6. The camphorsulfonate salt according to any one of claims 1 to 5, which is in crystalline solid form. 7. A crystalline solid form of the camphorsulfonate salt according to claim 6, characterized by at least one of the following: a) a DSC thermogram showing an endothermic peak with an onset at 163-166 Â°C, or b) an IR spectra showing the following bands at 2955, 1745, 1702, 1617, 1513, 1470, 1369, 1329, 1250, 1 162, 1051 , 1038, 852, 746, 700 cm "1 . 8. The camphorsulfonate salt according to any one of claims 1 to 5, which is in noncrystalline solid form. 9. A process for preparing the camphorsulfonate salt as defined in any one of claims 1 to 8, comprising the steps of: a) treating intermediate of formula (4) wherein PG is as defined in claim 1 or 5 a mixture of solvents isolating the camphorsulfonate salt obtained in step A process for the manufacture of olodaterol or a pharmaceutical salt thereof, preferably the R-enantiomer of olodaterol hydrochloride, comprising the steps of: d) providing camphorsulfonate salt of compound (4) as defined in any one of claims 1 to 8 e) optionally treating the camphorsulfonate salt of compound (4) with hydrochloric acid, to provide compound of formula (4)ÎÎ§, wherein HX is hydrochloric acid f) removing the hydroxyl protecting group PG from the products of steps a) or b), by hydrogenation in the presence of a catalyst and an organic solvent to provide olodaterol or a pharmaceutical salt thereof, preferably the R- enantiomer of olodaterol hydrochloride, and g) optionally, treating olodaterol obtained in step d) with a pharmaceutically acceptable acid to provide a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, preferably the R-enantiomer of olodaterol hydrochloride. 1 1 . A process according to claim 10, wherein the camphorsulfonate salt of compound (4) is obtained by a process comprising the steps of: a) reacting an amine of formula (3) or a salt thereof with epoxide of formula (2), wherein PG is a hydroxyl protecting group as defined in any one of claims 1 or 5, in the presence of an organic solvent to provide the compound of formula (4) b) treating the compound of formula (4) obtained in step a) with camphorsulfonic acid in the presence of a solvent or a mixture of solvents, wherein PG is a hydroxyl protecting group as defined in step a). 12. A 1 ,1-dimethyl-2-(4-methoxyphenyl)ethyl amine L-tartrate salt, compound of formula (3)-L-tartaric acid. (3)-L-tartaric acid 13. The L-tartrate salt according to claim 12, which is in crystalline solid form. 14. The L-tartrate salt according to claim 12, which is in non-crystalline solid form. 15. The L-tartrate salt according to anyone of claims 12 to 13, characterized in that the crystalline solid form is in anhydrous form. 16. The L-tartrate salt according to anyone of claims 13 and 15, characterized by a DSC thermogram showing a characteristic endothermic peak with an onset at 209-21 1 Â°C. 17. The L-tartrate salt according to anyone of claims 13, 15 and 16, characterized by an IR having the characteristics bands at 3386, 3283, 3207, 2995, 2956, 2908, 1648, 1507, 1252, 1 121 and 823 cm "1 . 18. A process for preparing the L-tartrate salt according to anyone of claims 12 to 17, which comprises the steps of: a) treating amine of formula (3) with L-tartaric acid in the presence of a solvent, preferably in the presence of a mixture of an organic solvent and water < 3 > , and b) isolating the L-tartrate salt obtained in step a). 19. The process according to claim 18, wherein the ratio of the organic solvent and water is from 10:1 to 1 :1 (v/v), preferably from 7:1 to 3:1 (v/v). 20. The process according to claim 18 and 19, wherein the organic solvent is an alcohol. 21 . The process according to claims 18 to 20, wherein the amine of formula (3) is prepared by a process comprising the steps of: a) treating the compound of formula (6) with a methylating agent in the presence of a base and an organic solvent, (6) to provide the compound of formula (7) < 7 > , and 

 b) converting the compound of formula (7) obtained in step a) in the amine of formula (3) by hydrogenation in the presence of a catalyst and an organic solvent. 22. A 1 ,1-dimethyl-2-(4-methoxyphenyl)ethyl amine maleate salt, compound of formula (3)-maleic acid 23. The maleate salt according to claim 22, wherein the molar ratio of 1 ,1-dimethyl- 2-(4-methoxyphenyl)ethyl amine to maleic acid is 2:1 . 24. The maleate salt according to claim 22, wherein the molar ratio of 1 ,1-dimethyl- 2-(4-methoxyphenyl)ethyl amine to maleic acid is 1 :1 . 25. The maleate salt according to any one of claims 22 to 24, which is in a crystalline solid form. 26. The maleate salt according to any one of claims 22 to 25, which is in a noncrystalline solid form. 27. A process for preparing the maleate salt according to anyone of claims 22 to 26, which comprises the steps of: a) treating amine of formula (3) with maleic acid in the presence of a solvent (3) , and b) isolating the maleate salt obtained in step a). 28. A process according to claim 1 1 , wherein the amine of formula (3) (3) is obtained by treating the L-tartrate salt as defined to anyone of claims 12 to 17 or obtainable through the process according to anyone of claims 18 to 21 , with a base in the presence of a mixture of an organic solvent and water to provide the amine of formula (3). . A process according to claim 1 1 , wherein the amine of formula (3) (3) is obtained by treating the maleate salt as defined to anyone of claims 22 to 26 or obtainable through the process according to claim 27, with a base in the presence of a mixture of an organic solvent and water. 30. The process according to anyone of claims 9 to 1 1 and 28-29, wherein the hydroxyl protecting group PG is an aralkyl group. 31 . The process according to anyone of claims 9 to 1 1 and 28-29, wherein the hydroxyl protecting group PG is C Cioalkyl substituted by a C 6 -C 10 aryl group. 32. The process according to anyone of claims 9 to 1 1 and 28-29, wherein the hydroxyl protecting group PG is benzyl or p-methoxybenzyl, preferably the hydroxyl protecting group PG is benzyl. 33. The use of the camphorsulfonate salt as defined in any one of claims 1 to 8 or obtainable through the process of claim 9, for preparing olodaterol or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, preferably the R-enantiomer of olodaterol hydrochloride. 34. The use of L-tartrate salt as defined in anyone of claims 12 to 17 or obtainable through the process according to anyone of claims 18 to 20, for preparing 

 olodaterol or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, preferably the R- enantiomer of olodaterol hydrochloride. 35. The use of the maleate salt as defined in any one of claims 22 to 26 or obtainable through the process according to claim 27, for preparing olodaterol or a pharmaceutically acceptable salt thereof, preferably the R-enantiomer of olodaterol hydrochloride.
